channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Over flowing bin, 14th June
item.description:
This Greenwich council bin has been dumped outside our flat. People are now just leaving their bin bags next to the bin. Please can it be removed / emptied before it attracts rats and other rodents. We believe the bin might belong to Bruford court estate .

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Creek Road, Deptford
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Granville Road, 14th June
item.description:
The areas around Granville and Chichester Road are constantly plagued with litter, I never see any street sweepers patrolling this area, it’s a total disgrace Brent Council. Please urgently address this as many residents have now reported this. We expect a street sweeper to attend these streets at least once per week. It would also help if you installed some litter bins in this area and also instruct your refuse collectors to pick up rubbish that they drop whilst emptying bins into the lorries
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Granville Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Street cleaning - Grass verges / Shrub beds - Littering

item.title: Dead fox cub on pavement, 14th June
item.description:
It’s dead and should be removed.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Towncourt Crescent, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ivorydown, Downham, Greater London

item.title: Rubbish dumped on pavement, 14th June
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Southport Road, Plumstead

item.title: Dumped clothing under the trees, 14th June
item.description:
Noticed Saturday 13 June 2026. Due to removal of clothing bank plus everything else someone has dumped clothing on the grass under the trees.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Jenner Close, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Warepoint Drive, Thamesmead

item.title: Foliage removal required, 14th June
item.description:
CAS-4491536-D3G6H9 - trees require cutting back
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Downham Way, Chinbrook, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A seedling sycamore growing very close to pavement, 14th June
item.description:
A short while ago I reported this seedling sycamore. Someone came and cut the top off. It is growing again. It needs digging out NOW. Cutting the growth is not enough. Get it out now before the roots establish themselves.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Grassington Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Potholes, 14th June
item.description:
There are many potholes in this area of the road some of which are deep and dangerous. Please can this be repaired as they are causing vehicle wear and tear.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southill Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car outside our house, 14th June
item.description:
This car has been outside our house and had not been moved in at least 6 months. I remember seeing the owner previously (we’ve been at the property for over 5 years) but they have since left their house. We never knew them, but their house is currently being renovated and we haven’t seen them in a long time.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tennyson Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned wheelie bin filled with rubbish that isn’t being collected, 14th June
item.description:
This bin has been here for months and doesn’t appear to belong to anyone. It’s gradually filled up with litter, some of it bagged, but it isn’t being collected or emptied. Someone has put a bag of garden waste next to it too.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wordsworth Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lane by Granville Recreation Allotments, 14th June
item.description:
Huge amount of fly tipped rubbish and debris lying in this area. This was done by the man living in the caravan at the end of Granville cul de sac when he had an encampment here, it’s such a mess and a total disgrace to all of us paying council tax. Please clean it up Brent Council and prevent these situations from happening again

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Granville Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: Overflowing street litter bin, 14th June
item.description:
The bin is on the corner of Place Farm Avenue just around the corner from Morrisons entrance. Rubbish is now being dumped alongside the bin

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Place Farm Avenue, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Stretch of road in front of side road too uneven, 14th June
item.description:
Too uneven road that has been left unsurfaced. The bikes and cars all shake crossing this section

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-3, Miles Street, Vauxhall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish, 14th June
item.description:
Rubbish

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 14th June
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24-25, Crescent Way, Ladywell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Granville Recreation Allotments lane, 14th June
item.description:
Homeless people have dumped a number of items from their previous encampment here. It has been here for months and nothing has been actioned by Brent Council, it’s a total disgrace to all of us paying council tax. Please clear this up and resolve the issue

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Granville Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load
